title: ISCC Canada CFR Recognized Under Clean Fuel Regulations as Approved Certification Scheme for Feedstock Compliance
content:
Environment and Climate Change Canada (ECCC) has officially recognized ISCC Canada CFR, a certification scheme operated by ISCC System GmbH, under section 62 of the Clean Fuel Regulations (CFR). This certification provides an optional mechanism for demonstrating that a feedstock used to produce low-carbon-intensity fuel meets the Land Use and Biodiversity (LUB) criteria outlined in sections 48â€“52 of the CFR.

This recognition specifically applies to feedstocks that are:

Eligible under paragraph 46(1)(c) of the CFR (e.g., agricultural or forest biomass),

Not excluded under paragraph 46(1)(b),

Certified as LUB-compliant.

To assert compliance, any party involved in handling the feedstock (e.g., processors, mixers, harvesters) must provide a declaration and a copy of the certificate to the fuel producer or purchaser. Fuel producers using these certified feedstocks must retain records of each declaration and certificate, per section 59(1). During regulatory verification, the fuel producerâ€™s verification body will review the certificates as evidence of LUB compliance.

Information related to this approval is now available on the CFR Google Drive under section 9.1 Certification. Stakeholders are encouraged to visit ISCCâ€™s official website for more information or reach out to cfsncp@ec.gc.ca for regulatory inquiries.

ECCC notes that this communication does not override the Clean Fuel Regulations or the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999, and any inconsistencies must defer to the official legislation.
author: Mansift Kaur
